reasoning_content: We need to check if 3937 is a prime number. Quick sanity check: check divisibility by small primes. 3937 is odd, not divisible by 2. Sum of digits: 3+9+3+7=22, not divisible by 3. Last digit not 0 or 5, so not divisible by 5. Check 7: 7*562=3934, remainder 3, so not divisible. 11: 11*358=3938, too high; 11*357=3927, remainder 10, so no. 13: 13*303=3939, too high; 13*302=3926, remainder 11, so no. 17: 17*231=3927, remainder 10; 17*232=3944 too high. 19: 19*207=3933, remainder 4; 19*208=3952. 23: 23*171=3933, remainder 4; 23*172=3956. 29: 29*135=3915, remainder 22; 29*136=3944. 31: 31*127=3937? 31*127 = 31*100=3100, 31*27=837, total 3937. Yes! 31 * 127 = 3937. So 3937 is composite (not prime). Therefore, sanity check: it's not prime. Answer: composite.
